sample.title: Why Gen Z Is Gambling With Its Future
sample.description:
With homeownership out of reach, artificial intelligence cutting job prospects and index funds feeling too slow, many young investors are turning to meme stocks, options and crypto to meet their financial goals. 

Read more on Bloomberg.com: 

Gen-Z Traders Go for Broke in Pursuit of a New American Dream
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2026-06-17/ai-meme-stock-gains-shape-gen-z-s-embrace-of-risk-assets

01:51 Risk mindset
03:49 ‘Financial Nihilism’
05:47 Gamification of trading
06:38 Alternative investments 
08:43 Misunderstanding Gen Z

sample.title: How China came to dominate the global EV factory boom
sample.description:
Chinese automakers have made over $100 billion in EV and battery investments abroad since 2019, according to Atlas Public Policy, far outpacing US firms. CNBC’s Robert Ferris has more on how the Asian country got here and why.

Chapter 1:  What is happening - 01:18
Chapter 2: Why this matters - 03:05
Chapter 3: The future - 06:00

sample.description:

Meet the YouTube whisperers, a booming class of advisors behind MrBeast and other million-dollar channels.

sample.title: Americans keep getting priced out of fun. Here's how it impacts us at home
sample.description:
"Funflation," the rising cost of concerts, sporting events, and travel, is surging across America. Now, even staying at home and doing activities like gaming and streaming is expensive. That's because of tariffs, a global memory shortage, and fewer customers. CNBC’s Alex Harring has the details.

sample.title: Will The New Housing Bill Lower Housing Costs?
sample.description:
The largest housing bill in decades has just become law after weeks of tense political standoff between President Trump and Congress. The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act focuses on three major reforms. It aims to increase the supply of housing by combining federal incentives for new homes with reforms designed to speed up home construction. The law also seeks to expand access to housing finance while limiting large institutional investors from buying single-family homes. Now comes the real test: can this ambitious legislation actually make housing affordable again?

Credits:
Reporter: Sarah Agostino, Mike Winters
Producer: Juhohn Lee
Editor: Meline Rosales
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Director of Video: Jessica Leibowitz
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: AI’s Next Race: Cost, Control, and Compute
sample.description:
The AI race is shifting from who has the biggest model to who can run, control and deploy AI most effectively.

Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins to discuss the company’s new orchestrator model, why he’s building on open-source Chinese AI, and his argument that “token value per watt” may decide the next phase of competition.

Then, Benchmark general partner Peter Fenton and Ollama CEO Jeff Morgan discuss the rise of open models, why enterprises are increasingly running models they can download and control, and what Ollama’s growth says about where the AI ecosystem is heading.

Together, the conversations get at one big question: in AI’s next race, will the winners be defined less by model access and more by cost, control and compute?

sample.title: Goldman Sachs reports record revenue that beat estimates by nearly $4 billion
sample.description: Goldman Sachs reported second-quarter earnings that blew past expectations. CNBC's Leslie Picker reports.

sample.title: Oil surges as Trump threatens 20% Hormuz transit toll
sample.description: Oil prices surged after President Trump threatened to impose a transit toll on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz, raising fresh concerns over global energy supplies. CNBC's Dan Murphy reports.

sample.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
sample.description: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts, directing all of this year’s donations to four family-linked foundations. CNBC's Becky Quick reports.

sample.title: Bank of America reports earnings beat, sees "resilient" consumer
sample.description:
Bank of America report second quarter earnings on Tuesday before the bell.

CEO Brian Moynihan said it was one of the strongest quarters to date for his company. “Every business segment reported double digit net income growth and strong returns on equity,” he said.

sample.title: Aftermath of deadly Bangkok pub fire
sample.description:
Relatives of victims who died in a devastating fire at a live music pub in Thailand's capital started to receive the bodies of their loved ones on July 14, while some were seen at the pub performing religious rites believed to bring the spirits home. Visual journalist Chalinee Thirasupa arrived to the scene on the night as rescue workers recovered the bodies of those caught in the blaze.

#bangkok #bangkokfire #fire #thailand

sample.title: New explosions near Iranian port cities, says state media | BBC News
sample.description:
Explosions have been heard near two Iranian port cities, Bandar Abbas and Bushehr, state media has reported.
 
It comes after another night of strikes between the US and Iran, with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps saying it hit two UAE tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and targeted US facilities in Jordan and Bahrain.
 
The UAE called the attack "brazen", adding that an Indian crew member was killed and eight others were injured.
 
Meanwhile, the US military says it completed strikes on targets aimed at degrading "Iran's ability to attack commercial shipping" - Iranian state media reports three people were killed.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#BBCNews

sample.title: Adam Ramsay-Peaty on marriage, pregnancy and Commonwealth Games | BBC News
sample.description:
More than a decade ago, Adam Ramsay-Peaty burst onto the scene at the Commonwealth Games and became one of the most successful swimmers in history. 

Life has changed quite a lot for Ramsay-Peaty as the 2026 Games approach - battling burnout, getting married and preparing to become a father for the second time. 

BBC Breakfast caught up with him ahead of his return to Glasgow.

sample.title: Bastille Day is France's independence day. #BastilleDay #Paris #BBCNews
UK at 'exceptional' risk of wildfires as two arrested over fires in France | BBC News
sample.description:
French firefighters are still battling two fires south of Paris. 
 
Almost 1,500 hectares of the Fontainebleau forest have been burned after the fire began on Sunday.
 
Two people have been arrested on suspicion of arson. 
 
Meanwhile, parts of the UK face an 'exceptional' risk for wildfires over the next few days as the latest heatwave continues, Natural England has warned.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews
